Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for emails related to the ExpenseBot auto-categorizer, expense audit, invoice incident, and AWS cost spike (via search_emails) to find Samira's audit summary to Elena ($885 exposure, 47 expenses reviewed), Marcus's invoice escalation about Sunbelt/Palmetto, Dmitri's audit findings, including Kenji Nakamura's false approval, and Elena's cost spike directives.
- List engineering team issues (via linear_list_issues with team="team_engineering") to find all current tickets, including ENG-205 through ENG-220, their states, assignees, and due dates - identifying which are in state_todo or state_in_progress but may actually be done, which are overdue, and which lack owners.
- Read comments on key engineering issues (via linear_list_comments for each issueId): issue_policy_config (ENG-202), issue_admin_dashboard (ENG-203), issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212), issue_p1_mosaic_malformed (ENG-215), and linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (ENG-187) to discover status updates not reflected in issue fields - Lena's "epic complete in staging" confirmation (ENG-205/206/207 done), Anh's dashboard features complete and Chloe approved (ENG-208 done), Lena's "config deployed, closing ENG-214" (ENG-214 done), Dmitri's 6 tests passing but remaining edge-case work (ENG-212 not done), Lena's ENG-187 deferral, and Marcus's April 15 and April 22 escalations demanding ENG-187 reactivation after the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident. Also read OPS-417 comments and details (via linear_list_comments with issueId="linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130" and/or linear_get_issue with id="linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130") — this issue has comments from Samira (engineering context on the dormant safeguard gap), David Chen (CE view framing this as the second pricing-exposure incident after Terraform), and Marcus (finance follow-up demanding engineering reactivate the invoice attachment safeguard and assign an owner). The issue description also contains the cross-reference to ENG-187.
- Read #engineering channel (C003) history (via conversations_history) to find team discussions and status updates related to the auto-categorizer pipeline, audit progress, and infrastructure work.
- Read #root-cause-aws-spike channel (C009) history (via conversations_history) to find the AWS cost spike investigation - Lena's infrastructure findings (EC2/S3/RDS metrics), Julian's confirmation of Vantage Distributed onboarding as root cause ($42,870 March bill), and Samira's remediation plan including P0 CloudWatch alarms and infrastructure cost estimator directives.
- Search QuickBooks for the March AWS bill (via quickbooks_search_bills with criteria targeting vendor Amazon Web Services and/or DocNumber AWS-2026-03-INV) to find bill record Id bill-aws-march-001 ($42,870, open balance). Note: stipend admin hold details (STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington $89, STIP-2026-0097 for Jordan Ekwueme $45) are discoverable via Marcus's email to Dmitri (OE 1 search_emails) and Airtable stipend records (OE 7 airtable_list_records), not via quickbooks_search_bills.
- List Airtable stipend records (via airtable_list_records with base_id="appMoveOpsOps001", table_name="Stipend Transactions") to find expense and stipend transaction details related to the audit — including false approval amounts, duplicate receipt flags, and admin hold statuses.
- Look up Elena Rostova's contact information (via contacts_search_contacts with query="Elena Rostova") to confirm elena.rostova@moveops.com as the recipient for the status email.
- Update stale engineering tickets to Done (via linear_update_issue with state="state_done"): ENG-205 (id=issue_category_matcher), ENG-206 (id=issue_policy_validator), ENG-207 (id=issue_decision_engine) - confirmed complete by Lena's comment on ENG-202; ENG-208 (id=issue_rule_config_ui) - confirmed complete by Anh's comment on ENG-203; ENG-214 (id=issue_p1_vectral_config) - confirmed deployed by Lena's comment on ENG-215; ENG-215 (id=issue_p1_mosaic_malformed) - completed alongside ENG-214; ENG-216 (id=issue_audit_expenses) - confirmed complete by Samira's audit summary email.
- Create a new engineering issue (via linear_create_issue) on team_engineering for AWS cost controls/infrastructure cost monitoring, formalizing the tracking gap for Elena's mandated P0 cost alert thresholds and infrastructure cost estimator that have no existing Linear tickets.
- Escalate ENG-187 (via linear_update_issue with id linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) - raise priority to P1/Urgent, assign an owner, or move out of backlog - in response to Marcus's demand that engineering reactivate the dormant invoice attachment safeguard work after the Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF-swap incident.
- Send an email (via send_email with sender="samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients=["elena.rostova@moveops.com"]) with a full engineering status summary covering: auto-categorizer pipeline progress, expense audit results ($885 exposure including Kenji Nakamura), stale tickets updated to Done, ENG-187 invoice safeguard gap and its connection to the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident (ownership split: ops owns remediation via OPS-417, engineering owns the safeguard via ENG-187), AWS cost spike root cause (Vantage Distributed, $42,870) and untracked follow-up controls, ENG-218 overdue status (Anh, dashboard type validation due 3/21), ENG-219 launch readiness checklist as relaunch blocker (Samira, due 3/21), and ENG-220 overdue status (Dmitri's duplicate detection due 3/25).
- Post a condensed status update (via conversations_add_message with channel_id="C003", payload as markdown) to #engineering covering ticket board cleanup, key blockers, ENG-187 urgency and need for an owner, ENG-219 launch readiness checklist as a relaunch gate, and heading into Friday priorities so the team knows where everything stands.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
